title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for prefetch, consumer concurrency, capacity, and fairness and requires the smallest defensible prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.
Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Prefetch Window Semantics · changed case · Quiz Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 2675 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Prefetch Window Semantics without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Prefetch Window Semantics · changed case · Quiz Prefetch Window Semantics.
